Big Data Analytics: SPARCS 2016 Hospital Inpatient Discharge Analysis

Samuel Abrokwah Mintah(22433576)

2026-07-17

Introduction

Background

Healthcare datasets contain large volumes of patient records that can provide valuable insights into healthcare utilization, patient outcomes, and hospital resource requirements.

This project analyses the Statewide Planning and Research Cooperative System (SPARCS) 2016 Hospital Inpatient Discharge Dataset from the New York State Department of Health.

The dataset contains approximately 2.3 million hospital discharge records with multiple demographic, clinical, and financial variables.

Due to the large size of the dataset, Apache Spark was used for distributed data processing and analysis.

Objectives

The objectives of this analysis are:

  1. Load and process a large healthcare dataset using Apache Spark.
  2. Perform data quality assessment and exploratory analysis.
  3. Examine relationships between hospital costs and patient characteristics.
  4. Develop regression models for predicting total hospital costs.
  5. Develop classification models for predicting home discharge.
  6. Identify important predictors influencing patient outcomes.

Dataset Description

The dataset used in this analysis is the SPARCS 2016 Hospital Inpatient Discharge Dataset, published by the New York State Department of Health. It contains de-identified, patient-level records for hospital inpatient discharges across New York State in 2016, comprising approximately 2.3 million observations across 34 variables. The dataset includes categorical variables (e.g., Gender, Race, Ethnicity, Type of Admission, Age Group) and numerical variables (e.g., Length of Stay, Total Charges, Total Costs, APR Severity of Illness Code), satisfying the project’s requirement of at least 500,000 observations with a minimum of two categorical and five numerical variables.

Citation (APA 7th edition):

New York State Department of Health. (2016). Hospital inpatient discharges (SPARCS de-identified): 2016 [Data set]. Health Data NY. https://health.data.ny.gov/Health/Hospital-Inpatient-Discharges-SPARCS-De-Identified/gnzp-ekau

1. Spark Setup and Data Loading

Required Packages

library(sparklyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidymodels)

Spark Environment Configuration

Sys.setenv(
  SPARK_HOME="/home/zionknight/spark/spark-3.5.8-bin-hadoop3",
  JAVA_HOME="/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64"
)

config <- spark_config()

# IMPORTANT:
# Removing these prevents the
# "--driver.memory" startup error.
config$sparklyr.shell.driver.memory <- NULL
config$sparklyr.shell.executor.memory <- NULL

Connect to Spark

sc <- spark_connect(
  master = "local[*]",
  config = config
)

spark_version(sc)
## [1] '3.5.8'
spark_connection_is_open(sc)
## [1] TRUE

Dataset Import

sp_csv <- spark_read_csv(
  sc,
  name = "sparcs_2016",
  path = "SPARCS_2016.csv",
  memory = FALSE,
  infer_schema = TRUE,
  header = TRUE
)

Dataset Dimensions

sdf_dim(sp_csv)
## [1] 2343429      34

Preview Dataset

head(sp_csv)
## # A query:  ?? x 34
## # Database: spark_connection
##   Health_Service_Area Hospital_County Operating_Certificate_Number Facility_Id

## 1 Western NY          Allegany                              226700 37         
## 2 Western NY          Allegany                              226700 37         
## 3 Western NY          Allegany                              226700 37         
## 4 Western NY          Allegany                              226700 37

## 6 Western NY          Allegany                              226700 37         
## # ℹ 30 more variables: Facility_Name <chr>, Age_Group <chr>,
## #   Zip_Code__3_digits <chr>, Gender <chr>, Race <chr>, Ethnicity <chr>,
## #   Length_of_Stay <chr>, Type_of_Admission <chr>, Patient_Disposition <chr>,
## #   Discharge_Year <int>, CCS_Diagnosis_Code <int>,
## #   CCS_Diagnosis_Description <chr>, CCS_Procedure_Code <int>,
## #   CCS_Procedure_Description <chr>, APR_DRG_Code <int>,
## #   APR_DRG_Description <chr>, APR_MDC_Code <int>, APR_MDC_Description <chr>, …

Data Cleaning and Preparation

The following variables require numeric conversion:

  • Length of Stay
  • Total Charges
  • Total Costs

Currency formatting characters are removed before conversion.

sparcs_clean <- sp_csv %>%
  mutate(
    
    Length_of_Stay = as.numeric(
      Length_of_Stay
    ),
    
    Total_Charges = as.numeric(
      regexp_replace(
        Total_Charges,
        "[^0-9.]",
        ""
      )
    ),
    
    Total_Costs = as.numeric(
      regexp_replace(
        Total_Costs,
        "[^0-9.]",
        ""
      )
    )
    
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Length_of_Stay)
  )

sparcs_clean <- sdf_persist(
  sparcs_clean,
  storage.level = "MEMORY_AND_DISK"
)

Verify Cleaning

sparcs_clean %>%
  select(
    Total_Charges,
    Total_Costs,
    Length_of_Stay
  ) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 10 × 3
##    Total_Charges Total_Costs Length_of_Stay
##            <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>
##  1         3913.       3467.              3
##  2         3597.       3747.              2
##  3         9149.       8322.              7
##  4         5881.       5829.              4
##  5         7020.       6604               5
##  6         2259.       2625.              1
##  7         3212.       3164.              2
##  8         4010.       3627.              3
##  9         4050.       3604.              3
## 10         4876.       4876.              3

2. Data Quality Assessment and Correlation Analysis

Overview

Data quality assessment was performed to identify missing values, duplicate records, invalid numerical values, and distributions of important categorical variables.

Missing Value Assessment

sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(is.na(Length_of_Stay)) %>%
  summarise(
    Missing = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   Missing
##     <dbl>
## 1       0
sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(is.na(Total_Charges)) %>%
  summarise(
    Missing = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   Missing
##     <dbl>
## 1       0
sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(is.na(Total_Costs)) %>%
  summarise(
    Missing = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   Missing
##     <dbl>
## 1       0
sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(is.na(Birth_Weight)) %>%
  summarise(
    Missing = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   Missing
##     <dbl>
## 1       0

Duplicate Record Assessment

total_rows <- sdf_nrow(
  sparcs_clean
)


total_rows
## [1] 2341676

Categorical Variable Assessment

Gender

sparcs_clean %>%
  distinct(Gender) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 3 × 1
##   Gender
##   <chr> 
## 1 F     
## 2 M     
## 3 U

Race

sparcs_clean %>%
  distinct(Race) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 4 × 1
##   Race                  
##   <chr>                 
## 1 Black/African American
## 2 Multi-racial          
## 3 Other Race            
## 4 White

Ethnicity

sparcs_clean %>%
  distinct(Ethnicity) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 4 × 1
##   Ethnicity        
##   <chr>            
## 1 Multi-ethnic     
## 2 Not Span/Hispanic
## 3 Spanish/Hispanic 
## 4 Unknown

Type of Admission

sparcs_clean %>%
  distinct(Type_of_Admission) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 6 × 1
##   Type_of_Admission
##   <chr>            
## 1 Elective         
## 2 Emergency        
## 3 Newborn          
## 4 Not Available    
## 5 Trauma           
## 6 Urgent

Severity of Illness

sparcs_clean %>%
  distinct(
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Description
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 5 × 1
##   APR_Severity_of_Illness_Description
##   <chr>                              
## 1 <NA>                               
## 2 Extreme                            
## 3 Major                              
## 4 Minor                              
## 5 Moderate

Numerical Range Checks

Negative Costs

sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(
    Total_Costs < 0
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    rows = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##    rows
##   <dbl>
## 1     0

Negative Charges

sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(
    Total_Charges < 0
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    rows = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##    rows
##   <dbl>
## 1     0

Negative Length of Stay

sparcs_clean %>%
  filter(
    Length_of_Stay < 0
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    rows = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##    rows
##   <dbl>
## 1     0

Correlation Analysis

Numerical variables were selected to examine relationships among clinical severity indicators, resource utilization variables, and hospital costs.

spark_numeric <- sparcs_clean %>%
  select(
    Length_of_Stay,
    CCS_Diagnosis_Code,
    CCS_Procedure_Code,
    APR_DRG_Code,
    APR_MDC_Code,
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
    Birth_Weight,
    Total_Charges,
    Total_Costs
  )

Pearson Correlation Matrix

Base R Correlation

numeric_df <- spark_numeric %>%
  collect()


cor_matrix <- cor(
  numeric_df,
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs",
  method = "pearson"
)


round(
  cor_matrix,
  2
)
##                              Length_of_Stay CCS_Diagnosis_Code
## Length_of_Stay                         1.00               0.11
## CCS_Diagnosis_Code                     0.11               1.00
## CCS_Procedure_Code                     0.06               0.13
## APR_DRG_Code                           0.07               0.44
## APR_MDC_Code                           0.09               0.46
## APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code           0.35              -0.20
## Birth_Weight                          -0.10               0.05
## Total_Charges                          0.69              -0.11
## Total_Costs                            0.70              -0.10
##                              CCS_Procedure_Code APR_DRG_Code APR_MDC_Code
## Length_of_Stay                             0.06         0.07         0.09
## CCS_Diagnosis_Code                         0.13         0.44         0.46
## CCS_Procedure_Code                         1.00         0.20         0.20
## APR_DRG_Code                               0.20         1.00         0.95
## APR_MDC_Code                               0.20         0.95         1.00
## APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code              -0.03        -0.11        -0.11
## Birth_Weight                               0.14         0.29         0.26
## Total_Charges                              0.03        -0.10        -0.08
## Total_Costs                                0.04        -0.10        -0.07
##                              APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code Birth_Weight
## Length_of_Stay                                       0.35        -0.10
## CCS_Diagnosis_Code                                  -0.20         0.05
## CCS_Procedure_Code                                  -0.03         0.14
## APR_DRG_Code                                        -0.11         0.29
## APR_MDC_Code                                        -0.11         0.26
## APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code                         1.00        -0.26
## Birth_Weight                                        -0.26         1.00
## Total_Charges                                        0.34        -0.13
## Total_Costs                                          0.33        -0.13
##                              Total_Charges Total_Costs
## Length_of_Stay                        0.69        0.70
## CCS_Diagnosis_Code                   -0.11       -0.10
## CCS_Procedure_Code                    0.03        0.04
## APR_DRG_Code                         -0.10       -0.10
## APR_MDC_Code                         -0.08       -0.07
## APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code          0.34        0.33
## Birth_Weight                         -0.13       -0.13
## Total_Charges                         1.00        0.92
## Total_Costs                           0.92        1.00

Spark Correlation

spark_cor_matrix <- ml_corr(
  spark_numeric,
  method = "pearson"
)


round(
  spark_cor_matrix,
  2
)
## # A tibble: 9 × 9
##   Length_of_Stay CCS_Diagnosis_Code CCS_Procedure_Code APR_DRG_Code APR_MDC_Code
##            <dbl>              <dbl>              <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1           1                  0.11               0.06         0.07         0.09
## 2           0.11               1                  0.13         0.44         0.46
## 3           0.06               0.13               1            0.2          0.2 
## 4           0.07               0.44               0.2          1            0.95
## 5           0.09               0.46               0.2          0.95         1   
## 6           0.35              -0.2               -0.03        -0.11        -0.11
## 7          -0.1                0.05               0.14         0.29         0.26
## 8           0.69              -0.11               0.03        -0.1         -0.08
## 9           0.7               -0.1                0.04        -0.1         -0.07
## # ℹ 4 more variables: APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code <dbl>, Birth_Weight <dbl>,
## #   Total_Charges <dbl>, Total_Costs <dbl>

Correlation Using corrr

corrr_matrix <- numeric_df %>%
  correlate(
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs",
    method = "pearson"
  )


corrr_matrix
## # A tibble: 9 × 10
##   term         Length_of_Stay CCS_Diagnosis_Code CCS_Procedure_Code APR_DRG_Code
##   <chr>                 <dbl>              <dbl>              <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1 Length_of_S…        NA                  0.106              0.0635       0.0720
## 2 CCS_Diagnos…         0.106             NA                  0.133        0.437 
## 3 CCS_Procedu…         0.0635             0.133             NA            0.202 
## 4 APR_DRG_Code         0.0720             0.437              0.202       NA     
## 5 APR_MDC_Code         0.0891             0.464              0.200        0.953 
## 6 APR_Severit…         0.350             -0.196             -0.0257      -0.108 
## 7 Birth_Weight        -0.0976             0.0548             0.145        0.295 
## 8 Total_Charg…         0.690             -0.105              0.0295      -0.103 
## 9 Total_Costs          0.704             -0.0962             0.0359      -0.0988
## # ℹ 5 more variables: APR_MDC_Code <dbl>, APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code <dbl>,
## #   Birth_Weight <dbl>, Total_Charges <dbl>, Total_Costs <dbl>

Correlation Heatmap

heatmap_data <- corrr_matrix %>%
  stretch()


heatmap_data %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(r)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = x,
      y = y,
      fill = r
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1,1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of Numerical Variables",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Pearson Correlation"
  )

Train-Test Split

The dataset was divided into training and testing datasets for subsequent machine learning models.

set.seed(6082026)


spark_split <- sdf_random_split(
  sparcs_clean,
  training = 0.8,
  test = 0.2,
  seed = 6082026
)


train_spark <- spark_split$training

test_spark <- spark_split$test


sdf_dim(train_spark)
## [1] 1873334      34
sdf_dim(test_spark)
## [1] 468342     34

rsample::initial_split()

library(rsample)

# rsample requires an in-memory (local) data frame, unlike sdf_random_split()
# which operates directly on the distributed Spark data frame.
# numeric_df (collected earlier for correlation analysis) is reused here.

set.seed(6082026)

rsample_split <- initial_split(
  numeric_df,
  prop = 0.8
)

train_rsample <- training(rsample_split)

test_rsample <- testing(rsample_split)

nrow(train_rsample)
## [1] 1873340
nrow(test_rsample)
## [1] 468336

Note: sdf_random_split() partitions data inside Spark’s distributed engine (scales to the full 2.3M rows without collecting to R), while rsample::initial_split() partitions an in-memory R data frame — appropriate for smaller, already-collected datasets.

3. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

Overview

Exploratory Data Analysis was performed to understand patient characteristics, admission patterns, financial variables, and clinical severity distributions within the SPARCS dataset.

Dataset Dimensions

sdf_dim(sparcs_clean)
## [1] 2341676      34

Summary Statistics

sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_LOS = mean(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Mean_Charges = mean(
      Total_Charges,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Mean_Costs = mean(
      Total_Costs,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Mean_Severity = mean(
      APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   Mean_LOS Mean_Charges Mean_Costs Mean_Severity
##      <dbl>        <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>
## 1     5.32       45156.     15376.          2.02

Length of Stay Statistics

sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    
    Mean_LOS = mean(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Min_LOS = min(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Max_LOS = max(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   Mean_LOS Min_LOS Max_LOS
##      <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1     5.32       1     119

Total Charges Statistics

sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    
    Mean_Charges = mean(
      Total_Charges,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Min_Charges = min(
      Total_Charges,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Max_Charges = max(
      Total_Charges,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   Mean_Charges Min_Charges Max_Charges
##          <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1       45156.        0.01    4795126.

Total Costs Statistics

sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    
    Mean_Costs = mean(
      Total_Costs,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Min_Costs = min(
      Total_Costs,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    Max_Costs = max(
      Total_Costs,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
  ) %>%
  collect()

##   Mean_Costs Min_Costs Max_Costs
##        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>
## 1     15376.         0  1727205.

Numerical Quantiles

The 75th percentile values were calculated to understand the upper range of hospital stay and financial variables.

sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    
    LOS_75th = percentile_approx(
      Length_of_Stay,
      0.75
    ),
    
    Cost_75th = percentile_approx(
      Total_Costs,
      0.75
    ),
    
    Charges_75th = percentile_approx(
      Total_Charges,
      0.75
    )
    
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   LOS_75th Cost_75th Charges_75th
##      <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1        6    16680.       49285.

Home Discharge Distribution

A binary outcome variable was created for classification modelling.

Patients discharged to Home or Self Care were classified as “Yes”, while all other discharge destinations were classified as “No”.

sparcs_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Discharged_Home = ifelse(
      Patient_Disposition == "Home or Self Care",
      "Yes",
      "No"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    Discharged_Home
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = n()
  ) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   Discharged_Home   Count
##   <chr>             <dbl>
## 1 No               779946
## 2 Yes             1561730

4. Predictive Modelling

Two predictive modelling approaches were developed:

  1. Regression models to predict Total Hospital Costs
  2. Classification models to predict Home Discharge

Automated Regression Function

A reusable function that automates fitting, prediction, and evaluation for any Spark linear regression model:

run_spark_regression <- function(
  train_data,
  test_data,
  formula,
  label_col
) {

  model <- ml_linear_regression(
    train_data,
    formula
  )

  predictions <- ml_predict(
    model,
    test_data
  )

  # Extract label column name from formula
  label <- as.character(formula)[2]

  # Collect predictions and compute metrics manually
  pred_collected <- predictions %>%
    select(
      all_of(label),
      prediction
    ) %>%
    collect()

  rmse <- pred_collected %>%
    summarise(
      rmse = sqrt(
        mean((!!sym(label) - prediction)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    ) %>%
    pull(rmse)

  # Compute R2: 1 - (SS_res / SS_tot)
  ss_res <- pred_collected %>%
    summarise(
      ss_res = sum((!!sym(label) - prediction)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    pull(ss_res)

  ss_tot <- pred_collected %>%
    summarise(
      ss_tot = sum(
        (!!sym(label) - mean(!!sym(label), na.rm = TRUE))^2,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    ) %>%
    pull(ss_tot)

  r2 <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)

  list(
    model = model,
    coefficients = tidy(model),
    predictions = predictions,
    rmse = rmse,
    r2 = r2
  )

}

Apply Automated Function — Simple Linear Regression

slr_results <- run_spark_regression(
  train_spark,
  test_spark,
  Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay,
  "Total_Costs"
)

slr_results$rmse
## [1] 18852.38
slr_results$r2
## [1] 0.4877601

Apply Automated Function — Multiple Linear Regression

multiple_results <- run_spark_regression(
  train_spark,
  test_spark,
  Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay + APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
  "Total_Costs"
)

multiple_results$rmse
## [1] 18692.42
multiple_results$r2
## [1] 0.4964158

Regression Analysis

Simple Linear Regression

Predicting total hospital costs using length of stay.

slr_model <- ml_linear_regression(
  train_spark,
  Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay
)


slr_model
## Formula: Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay
## 
## Coefficients:
##    (Intercept) Length_of_Stay 
##       2219.652       2471.747
slr_coefficients <- tidy(
  slr_model
)


slr_coefficients
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   term           estimate std.error statistic p.value
##   <chr>             <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)       2220.     16.4       135.       0
## 2 Length_of_Stay    2472.      1.82     1362.       0

Standardization

Standardize predictors using training-set statistics only, to avoid data leakage:

train_stats <- train_spark %>%
  summarise(
    mean_LOS = mean(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    sd_LOS = sd(
      Length_of_Stay,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    mean_Severity = mean(
      APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    sd_Severity = sd(
      APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  collect()

train_spark_scaled <- train_spark %>%
  mutate(
    Length_of_Stay_scaled = (
      Length_of_Stay - !!train_stats$mean_LOS
    ) / !!train_stats$sd_LOS,
    Severity_scaled = (
      APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code - !!train_stats$mean_Severity
    ) / !!train_stats$sd_Severity
  )

test_spark_scaled <- test_spark %>%
  mutate(
    Length_of_Stay_scaled = (
      Length_of_Stay - !!train_stats$mean_LOS
    ) / !!train_stats$sd_LOS,
    Severity_scaled = (
      APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code - !!train_stats$mean_Severity
    ) / !!train_stats$sd_Severity
  )

multiple_model_scaled <- ml_linear_regression(
  train_spark_scaled,
  Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay_scaled + Severity_scaled
)

tidy(multiple_model_scaled)
## # A tibble: 3 × 5
##   term                  estimate std.error statistic p.value
##   <chr>                    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)             15367.      13.2     1168.       0
## 2 Length_of_Stay_scaled   17170.      14.0     1222.       0
## 3 Severity_scaled          2595.      14.0      185.       0

Multiple Linear Regression

Predicting total hospital costs using:

  • Length of Stay
  • Severity of Illness
multiple_model <- ml_linear_regression(
  train_spark,
  Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay +
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code
)


multiple_coefficients <- tidy(
  multiple_model
)


multiple_coefficients
## # A tibble: 3 × 5
##   term                         estimate std.error statistic p.value
##   <chr>                           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)                    -2941.     32.3      -91.0       0
## 2 Length_of_Stay                  2348.      1.92    1222.        0
## 3 APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code    2877.     15.6      185.        0

Parsnip Comparison

library(parsnip)
library(yardstick)

# parsnip/tidymodels fits locally, so a sample is collected from Spark
regression_sample <- train_spark %>%
  select(
    Total_Costs,
    Length_of_Stay,
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code
  ) %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.05,
    seed = 6082026
  ) %>%
  collect()

test_sample_local <- test_spark %>%
  select(
    Total_Costs,
    Length_of_Stay,
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code
  ) %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.05,
    seed = 6082026
  ) %>%
  collect()

lm_parsnip_fit <- linear_reg() %>%
  set_engine("lm") %>%
  fit(
    Total_Costs ~ Length_of_Stay + APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
    data = regression_sample
  )

tidy(lm_parsnip_fit)
## # A tibble: 3 × 5
##   term                         estimate std.error statistic  p.value
##   <chr>                           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)                    -2864.    138.       -20.8 1.37e-95
## 2 Length_of_Stay                  2326.      8.28     281.  0       
## 3 APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code    2907.     66.5       43.7 0
parsnip_predictions <- predict(
  lm_parsnip_fit,
  new_data = test_sample_local
) %>%
  bind_cols(test_sample_local)

parsnip_rmse <- parsnip_predictions %>%
  rmse(
    truth = Total_Costs,
    estimate = .pred
  )

# Recreate Spark predictions fresh to avoid stale reference
spark_predictions_fresh <- ml_predict(
  multiple_model,
  test_spark
)

spark_rmse <- spark_predictions_fresh %>%
  select(
    Total_Costs,
    prediction
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    squared_error = (Total_Costs - prediction)^2
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    mse = mean(squared_error, na.rm = TRUE),
    rmse = sqrt(mse)
  ) %>%
  pull(rmse)

model_comparison <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Spark (ml_linear_regression)",
    "Parsnip (lm engine)"
  ),
  RMSE = c(
    spark_rmse,
    parsnip_rmse$.estimate
  )
)

model_comparison
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   Model                          RMSE
##   <chr>                         <dbl>
## 1 Spark (ml_linear_regression) 18692.
## 2 Parsnip (lm engine)          19194.

Regression Predictions

regression_predictions <- ml_predict(
  multiple_model,
  test_spark
)


regression_predictions %>%
  select(
    Total_Costs,
    prediction
  ) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  collect()
## # A tibble: 10 × 2
##    Total_Costs prediction
##          <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1       8525.     12204.
##  2       3369.      9326.
##  3       2600.      4631.
##  4       4745.     12204.
##  5       1737.      4631.
##  6       1037.      4631.
##  7       4507.      9856.
##  8       3677.      9856.
##  9        598.      4631.
## 10       6034.      9856.

Regression Diagnostics

regression_predictions %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.01
  ) %>%
  select(
    prediction,
    Total_Costs
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = prediction,
      y = Total_Costs
    )
  ) +
  geom_point(
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    intercept = 0,
    slope = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title =
      "Observed vs Predicted Hospital Costs",
    x =
      "Predicted Costs",
    y =
      "Actual Costs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Classification Analysis

Create Classification Dataset

Patients discharged to Home or Self Care were classified as “Yes”.

classification_data <- sparcs_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Discharged_Home =
      ifelse(
        Patient_Disposition ==
          "Home or Self Care",
        "Yes",
        "No"
      )
  ) %>%
  select(
    Discharged_Home,
    Length_of_Stay,
    Total_Charges,
    Total_Costs,
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
    Age_Group,
    Gender,
    Race,
    Ethnicity,
    Type_of_Admission
  )

Classification Sample

A sample was used because tree-based models require more memory.

classification_sample <- classification_data %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.1
  )


class_split <- sdf_random_split(
  classification_sample,
  training = 0.8,
  test = 0.2,
  seed = 6082026
)


class_train_spark <- class_split$training

class_test_spark <- class_split$test


sdf_dim(class_train_spark)
## [1] 187075     10
sdf_dim(class_test_spark)
## [1] 46489    10

Logistic Regression

logistic_model <- ml_logistic_regression(
  class_train_spark,
  Discharged_Home ~ .
)


logistic_model
## Formula: Discharged_Home ~ .
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  (Intercept)               Length_of_Stay 
##                -3.494109e+00                 2.328253e-02 
##                Total_Charges                  Total_Costs 
##                 1.620378e-06                 8.187884e-06 
## APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code           Age_Group_50 to 69 
##                 5.648921e-01                 1.161643e+00 
##        Age_Group_70 or Older           Age_Group_30 to 49 
##                 2.153174e+00                 3.647144e-01 
##            Age_Group_0 to 17                     Gender_F 
##                -3.089339e-01                 6.503472e-03 
##                     Gender_M                   Race_White 
##                -1.000568e-02                -5.217256e-03 
##              Race_Other Race  Race_Black/African American 
##                -1.706211e-01                 7.599428e-04 
##  Ethnicity_Not Span/Hispanic   Ethnicity_Spanish/Hispanic 
##                 1.107557e-01                 7.681303e-03 
##            Ethnicity_Unknown  Type_of_Admission_Emergency 
##                 1.009296e-01                 7.322781e-02 
##   Type_of_Admission_Elective    Type_of_Admission_Newborn 
##                 2.989531e-01                -4.160444e-01 
##     Type_of_Admission_Urgent     Type_of_Admission_Trauma 
##                -1.497482e-01                 6.022390e-01

Random Forest

rf_model <- ml_random_forest_classifier(
  class_train_spark,
  Discharged_Home ~ .,
  num_trees = 50,
  max_depth = 10
)


rf_model
## Formula: Discharged_Home ~ .
## 
## RandomForestClassificationModel: uid=random_forest_classifier__4a367118_a988_4645_bfb3_7c034f2d4996, numTrees=50, numClasses=2, numFeatures=21

Gradient Boosted Trees

gbt_model <- ml_gbt_classifier(
  class_train_spark,
  Discharged_Home ~ .,
  max_iter = 20
)


gbt_model
## Formula: Discharged_Home ~ .
## 
## GBTClassificationModel: uid = gbt_classifier__a803ea8b_5306_4ccd_a6d7_b7c1a8e86b3c, numTrees=20, numClasses=2, numFeatures=21

Classification Predictions

Model Predictions

logistic_predictions <- ml_predict(
  logistic_model,
  class_test_spark
)

rf_predictions <- ml_predict(
  rf_model,
  class_test_spark
)

gbt_predictions <- ml_predict(
  gbt_model,
  class_test_spark
)

Accuracy Comparison

calculate_accuracy <- function(
  predictions,
  model_name
){

  results <- predictions %>%
    select(
      Discharged_Home,
      predicted_label
    ) %>%
    collect()


  results %>%
    summarise(
      Accuracy = mean(
        as.character(Discharged_Home) == as.character(predicted_label)
      ),
      Model = model_name
    )

}


full_accuracy_results <- bind_rows(

  calculate_accuracy(
    logistic_predictions,
    "Logistic Regression"
  ),

  calculate_accuracy(
    rf_predictions,
    "Random Forest"
  ),

  calculate_accuracy(
    gbt_predictions,
    "Gradient Boosted Trees"
  )

)


full_accuracy_results
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   Accuracy Model                 
##      <dbl> <chr>                 
## 1    0.764 Logistic Regression   
## 2    0.784 Random Forest         
## 3    0.783 Gradient Boosted Trees

Feature Importance

rf_importance <- ml_feature_importances(
  rf_model
)


rf_importance
##                         feature   importance
## 1         Age_Group_70 or Older 0.3049292736
## 2  APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code 0.1987423453
## 3                Length_of_Stay 0.1290405034
## 4                   Total_Costs 0.1123763337
## 5                 Total_Charges 0.0815026165
## 6            Age_Group_50 to 69 0.0457724509
## 7             Age_Group_0 to 17 0.0336764037
## 8            Age_Group_30 to 49 0.0272880812
## 9     Type_of_Admission_Newborn 0.0150971369
## 10  Type_of_Admission_Emergency 0.0149655306
## 11   Type_of_Admission_Elective 0.0073353596
## 12                     Gender_F 0.0070589498
## 13                     Gender_M 0.0064476651
## 14                   Race_White 0.0038274641
## 15              Race_Other Race 0.0029163869
## 16  Ethnicity_Not Span/Hispanic 0.0022110776
## 17   Ethnicity_Spanish/Hispanic 0.0021054458
## 18     Type_of_Admission_Urgent 0.0016794899
## 19  Race_Black/African American 0.0016540908
## 20            Ethnicity_Unknown 0.0008834715
## 21     Type_of_Admission_Trauma 0.0004899231

Feature Importance Plot

rf_importance %>%
  collect() %>%
  arrange(
    desc(importance)
  ) %>%
  slice_head(
    n = 10
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x =
        reorder(
          feature,
          importance
        ),
      y =
        importance
    )
  ) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title =
      "Top 10 Predictors of Home Discharge",
    x =
      "Feature",
    y =
      "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

5. Visualizations and Final Results

Correlation Heatmap

numeric_df <- sparcs_clean %>%
  select(
    Length_of_Stay,
    CCS_Diagnosis_Code,
    CCS_Procedure_Code,
    APR_DRG_Code,
    APR_MDC_Code,
    APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code,
    Birth_Weight,
    Total_Charges,
    Total_Costs
  ) %>%
  collect()


cor_matrix <- numeric_df %>%
  correlate(
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs",
    method = "pearson"
  )


heatmap_data <- cor_matrix %>%
  stretch()


heatmap_data %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(r)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = x,
      y = y,
      fill = r
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1,1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x =
      element_text(
        angle = 45,
        hjust = 1
      )
  ) +
  labs(
    title =
      "Correlation Heatmap of Numerical Variables",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

Length of Stay Distribution

sparcs_clean %>%
  select(
    Length_of_Stay
  ) %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.05
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = Length_of_Stay
    )
  ) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 50
  ) +
  labs(
    title =
      "Distribution of Length of Stay",
    x =
      "Length of Stay (Days)",
    y =
      "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Total Costs Distribution

sparcs_clean %>%
  select(
    Total_Costs
  ) %>%
  sdf_sample(
    fraction = 0.05
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = Total_Costs
    )
  ) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 50
  ) +
  labs(
    title =
      "Distribution of Total Hospital Costs",
    x =
      "Total Costs",
    y =
      "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Classification Accuracy Comparison

full_accuracy_results %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = Model,
      y = Accuracy
    )
  ) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title =
      "Classification Model Accuracy Comparison",
    x =
      "Model",
    y =
      "Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Boosted Tree Feature Importance

rf_importance %>%
  collect() %>%
  arrange(
    desc(importance)
  ) %>%
  slice_head(
    n = 10
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x =
        reorder(
          feature,
          importance
        ),
      y =
        importance
    )
  ) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title =
      "Top 10 Predictors of Home Discharge",
    x =
      "Feature",
    y =
      "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

6. Final Summary

Dataset Overview

dataset_summary <- sparcs_clean %>%
  summarise(
    
    Total_Records = n(),
    
    Total_Variables = 34,
    
    Average_Length_of_Stay =
      mean(
        Length_of_Stay,
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
    
    Average_Total_Costs =
      mean(
        Total_Costs,
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
    
    Average_Total_Charges =
      mean(
        Total_Charges,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    
  ) %>%
  collect()


dataset_summary
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   Total_Records Total_Variables Average_Length_of_Stay Average_Total_Costs
##           <dbl>           <dbl>                  <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1       2341676              34                   5.32              15376.
## # ℹ 1 more variable: Average_Total_Charges <dbl>

Regression Results

regression_summary <-
  multiple_coefficients

regression_summary
## # A tibble: 3 × 5
##   term                         estimate std.error statistic p.value
##   <chr>                           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)                    -2941.     32.3      -91.0       0
## 2 Length_of_Stay                  2348.      1.92    1222.        0
## 3 APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code    2877.     15.6      185.        0

Classification Results

classification_summary <-
  full_accuracy_results %>%
  arrange(
    desc(Accuracy)
  )


classification_summary
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   Accuracy Model                 
##      <dbl> <chr>                 
## 1    0.784 Random Forest         
## 2    0.783 Gradient Boosted Trees
## 3    0.764 Logistic Regression

Feature Importance Summary

feature_importance_summary <-
  rf_importance %>%
  collect() %>%
  arrange(
    desc(importance)
  )


feature_importance_summary
##                         feature   importance
## 1         Age_Group_70 or Older 0.3049292736
## 2  APR_Severity_of_Illness_Code 0.1987423453
## 3                Length_of_Stay 0.1290405034
## 4                   Total_Costs 0.1123763337
## 5                 Total_Charges 0.0815026165
## 6            Age_Group_50 to 69 0.0457724509
## 7             Age_Group_0 to 17 0.0336764037
## 8            Age_Group_30 to 49 0.0272880812
## 9     Type_of_Admission_Newborn 0.0150971369
## 10  Type_of_Admission_Emergency 0.0149655306
## 11   Type_of_Admission_Elective 0.0073353596
## 12                     Gender_F 0.0070589498
## 13                     Gender_M 0.0064476651
## 14                   Race_White 0.0038274641
## 15              Race_Other Race 0.0029163869
## 16  Ethnicity_Not Span/Hispanic 0.0022110776
## 17   Ethnicity_Spanish/Hispanic 0.0021054458
## 18     Type_of_Admission_Urgent 0.0016794899
## 19  Race_Black/African American 0.0016540908
## 20            Ethnicity_Unknown 0.0008834715
## 21     Type_of_Admission_Trauma 0.0004899231

Final Conclusions

## 
## FINAL FINDINGS
## ==============
## 
## 
## 1. DATA PROCESSING
## 
## The SPARCS 2016 Hospital Inpatient Discharge Dataset contained over
## 2.3 million hospital discharge records.
## 
## Apache Spark provided an efficient framework for processing and analysing
## the large healthcare dataset.
## 
## 
## 2. EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS
## 
## The analysis examined patient demographics, admission characteristics,
## hospital costs, length of stay, and severity of illness.
## 
## Financial variables such as total charges and total costs showed important
## relationships with patient utilization patterns.
## 
## 
## 3. REGRESSION ANALYSIS
## 
## Regression models demonstrated that length of stay was an important predictor
## of total hospital costs.
## 
## The multiple regression model showed that adding severity of illness improved
## the ability to explain variation in hospital costs.
## 
## 
## 4. CLASSIFICATION ANALYSIS
## 
## Machine learning models were developed to predict whether patients were
## discharged home.
## 
## The following algorithms were compared:
## 
## - Logistic Regression
## - Gradient Boosted Trees
## - Random Forest
## 
## 
## The tree-based models provided improved predictive performance compared with
## the logistic regression baseline. Random Forest achieved 78.60% accuracy, 
## Gradient Boosted Trees achieved 78.23% accuracy, compared to logistic regression's 76.55% accuracy.
## 
## 
## 5. IMPORTANT PREDICTORS
## 
## Feature importance analysis identified variables associated with discharge
## outcomes, including:
## 
## - Severity of illness
## - Length of stay
## - Patient characteristics
## - Hospital cost variables
## 
## 
## 6. OVERALL CONCLUSION
## 
## The project demonstrates how Apache Spark and machine learning techniques
## can be applied to large healthcare datasets.
## 
## The Spark-based analytics pipeline successfully processed millions of
## records and extracted useful information regarding hospital costs and
## patient outcomes.

End of Report
